title: Interactive orientation (phase 1)
qaNote: All 5 ACs verified; build + tsc pass; canned copy accurate vs SKILL.md.
userStory: As a new user in phase 1, I want to explore the tier model by clicking cards and preview what a daily trigger phrase does before I type it, so that I understand the OS before committing to the setup steps.

acceptanceCriteria.text: Each of the three tier cards (Tier 1 · Constitution, Tier 2 · Living context, Tier 3 · Deep files) is clickable and renders a selected state (e.g. distinct border-primary ring or highlighted background). Clicking a card marks it selected and makes its files list and full description prominently visible. Only one card may be highlighted at a time. `grep -n 'selectedTier\|activeTier\|setSelectedTier' work-hq/src/routes/onboarding.tsx` returns at least one match inside the `PhaseOrientation` function body.

Tier cards are keyboard accessible: each renders as a `<button>` element or carries `tabIndex={0}` plus `role='button'` and an `onKeyDown` handler for Enter and Space. `grep -n 'onKeyDown\|role=.button\|tabIndex' work-hq/src/routes/onboarding.tsx` returns at least one match inside the `PhaseOrientation` function body.

Clicking any trigger-phrase chip ('morning standup', 'process my backlog', 'end session') displays a canned assistant response in a visible mini-chat panel below the chips section. The panel shows the clicked phrase as the user turn and the canned response as the assistant turn. No real assistant call is made. `grep -n 'cannedResponse\|miniChat\|chatPanel\|cannedReplies' work-hq/src/routes/onboarding.tsx` returns at least one match.

The privacy/offline note ('Your data stays local and works offline') remains visible at a viewport width of 1 280 px without vertical scrolling — the story 002 regression guard. Open `/onboarding` (phase 1) at 1 280×800; the note element is within the initial viewport. `grep -n 'stays local\|works offline' work-hq/src/routes/onboarding.tsx` returns a match confirming the note text is still present.

Phase 1 collects no user input and calls neither `set()` nor `saveOnboarding`. `grep -n '<input\|<textarea\|<select' work-hq/src/routes/onboarding.tsx` returns no match whose line number falls inside the `PhaseOrientation` function body. The Next phase button advances to phase 2 without requiring any interaction with the tier cards or mini-chat.

technicalNotes: Edit `PhaseOrientation` in `work-hq/src/routes/onboarding.tsx` (lines ~406–511). The `tiers` array (lines ~407–434) currently renders static `<div>` cards — convert each to a `<button>` and track `selectedTier: number | null` in local `useState`. The `triggers` array (line ~436) currently renders `<code>` chips — convert each to a `<button>`. Add a `cannedReplies: Record<string, string>` constant with one short canned response per trigger phrase describing what that ritual does (source: os-helper SKILL.md Modes). Track `chatHistory: Array<{ role: 'user'|'assistant'; text: string }>` in local state and render conditionally below the triggers section when non-empty. The privacy note (lines ~447–457) must stay above the interactive tier cards so it remains in the viewport at 1 280 px; do not reorder elements above it. Phase 1 must not call `set()`, `saveNow()`, or `saveOnboarding` — it is read-only teach content.
outOfScope: Making trigger-phrase chips actually invoke the assistant. Collecting any user answers or answers state changes in phase 1. Changing the tier model content (file paths, badge labels, or descriptions). Animating the mini-chat panel.

verificationPlan.checks: Returns at least one match inside the PhaseOrientation block confirming click-to-explore state is implemented.
Returns at least one match confirming the canned-response map and mini-chat panel state are present.
Returns a match confirming the privacy/offline note text is still present in PhaseOrientation.

qaReport.commitNote: impl.commit records 3cb8ded (pre-amend); authoritative commit verified as 598bdd4 — cosmetic discrepancy only, does not affect verdict.

perAc.ac: AC1 — Three tier cards clickable, selected state, one at a time, re-click deselects, selected reveals desc+chips, compact with affordance.
perAc.evidence: grep -n 'selectedTier|activeTier|setSelectedTier' → 3 matches at lines 671, 732, 741 (all inside PhaseOrientation which spans 637–851). PhaseOrientation function body confirmed via `grep -n 'function Phase'` (next fn PhaseSeeding starts at 1000). Diff shows real <button> elements with `aria-pressed={selected}`, `onClick={() => setSelectedTier(selected ? null : i)}` (toggle — re-click deselects), selected branch renders desc + file chips, unselected branch renders 'Click to explore' affordance. `selectedTier: number | null` ensures at most one selected. FOCUS_RING applied (line 745).

perAc.ac: AC2 — Tier cards keyboard accessible: <button> or tabIndex+role+onKeyDown for Enter/Space.
perAc.evidence: grep -n 'onKeyDown|role=.button|tabIndex' → onKeyDown at line 742 inside PhaseOrientation. Cards are real `<button type='button'>` elements (native Enter/Space handling). onTierKeyDown (lines 676–687) adds ArrowDown/ArrowUp/Home/End roving focus via tierRefs array; preventDefault only on those keys — Tab is never intercepted so focus cannot trap. Developer note that grep checks semantic intent (keyboard operability) not impl guess: verified. aria-pressed={selected} on each card.

perAc.ac: AC3 — Trigger chips show canned user+assistant turns in mini-chat panel; switching replaces; no real assistant call.
perAc.evidence: grep -n 'cannedResponse|miniChat|chatPanel|cannedReplies' → matches at lines 628 (module-level const cannedReplies: Record<string,string>) and 698 (used inside playTrigger). chatHistory state at line 695 (inside PhaseOrientation). playTrigger() at lines 696–699 calls setChatHistory([{role:'user',text:phrase},{role:'assistant',text:cannedReplies[phrase]}]) — replaces panel on every call (switch replaces). Mini-chat panel at lines 818–846 with aria-live='polite'. Canned copy cross-checked against skills/chief-of-staff/SKILL.md: standup reads context/active.md, returns top-3 priorities (title/owner/due date)+blockers+first action ✅; process-backlog reads BACKLOG.md, routes actions→active.md, project-notes→projects/, learnings→memory/learnings.md, reference→knowledge/this-week.md, clears BACKLOG.md ✅; end-session appends 1–3 patterns to memory/learnings.md, records decision in memory/decisions.md, logs row in memory/usage-log.md ✅. No saveOnboarding/set()/fetch inside PhaseOrientation.

perAc.ac: AC4 — Privacy/offline note visible at 1280px without scrolling; story 002 regression guard.
perAc.evidence:
grep -n 'stays local|works offline' → line 716 inside PhaseOrientation. DOM order confirmed by reading file lines 702–727: privacy note div (lines 713–722) renders immediately after the intro

and before the tier cards section (lines 724+). Privacy note is therefore the second element in the scroll area, well within any 1280x800 viewport. Story 002 regression guard preserved.

perAc.ac: AC5 — No user input, no set()/saveOnboarding in PhaseOrientation; Next advances without requiring tier/chat interaction.
perAc.evidence: grep -n '<input|<textarea|<select' → first match at line 1167 (inside PhaseSeeding, which starts at 1000 — after PhaseOrientation ends at 851). grep -n 'set(|saveNow|saveOnboarding' → all matches inside OnboardingPage (lines 419, 452, 454) or PhaseSeeding+ (lines 1072+); none in PhaseOrientation body. nextDisabled logic at line 468: `(phase === 2 && !allWireChecks) || (phase === 3 && !answers.seedingPath)` — phase 1 evaluates false on both clauses; Next button always enabled in phase 1.

qaReport.securityAndMigrationGates: Secrets scan: PASS — no API keys, tokens, passwords, or JWT-shaped strings in diff. Migration check: N/A — no schema changes.
qaReport.riskNotes: aria-live='polite' region is conditionally rendered (mounts only when chatHistory.length > 0). When a user clicks a chip the first time, the region mounts with content already present rather than pre-existing in the DOM. Modern browser+screen-reader pairs (Chrome+NVDA, Safari+VoiceOver) generally handle this, but some legacy combinations may miss the announcement. Not an AC violation; flagged for awareness if a11y audit is conducted.
qaReport.recommendedFollowUps: Consider pre-mounting the aria-live region (empty) so first-click announcements are more reliably announced across all screen reader combinations — could be addressed in a follow-up a11y hardening story for f-onboarding-ux.

impl.notes: PhaseOrientation rebuilt as interactive teach content, all local UI state — no set()/saveNow()/saveOnboarding calls, no persistence (AC5). AC1/AC2: the three tier cards are now real <button> elements toggling a selectedTier (number | null) local state; the highlighted card gains border-primary/60 bg-primary/5 ring-1 ring-primary/40 and reveals its description + file chips, unselected cards stay compact with a 'Click to explore' affordance, only one highlighted at a time (toggle re-click deselects). Keyboard: native buttons handle Enter/Space; added onTierKeyDown for ArrowUp/ArrowDown/Home/End roving focus across the cards via a tierRefs button-ref array (preventDefault only on arrows/Home/End so it never conflicts with the native click) — satisfies AC2's onKeyDown grep honestly without a double-fire on the toggle. aria-pressed reports selection on both tier cards and trigger chips. AC3: added module-level cannedReplies: Record<string,string> with accurate canned copy per phrase (chief-of-staff standup reads context/active.md; process-my-backlog reads BACKLOG.md and routes to active.md/projects/memory/knowledge then clears it; end-session harvest appends to memory/learnings.md, memory/decisions.md, memory/usage-log.md — sourced from skills/chief-of-staff/SKILL.md). Trigger chips are now buttons; clicking one sets chatHistory (Array<{role,text}>) to a user turn (the phrase) + assistant turn (the canned reply); switching phrase replaces the panel. Mini-chat panel renders below the chips only when non-empty, aria-live=polite, right-aligned primary user bubble / left-aligned muted assistant bubble, with a 'Preview only — no assistant was called' footnote. AC4: privacy/offline note kept early in DOM order (immediately after the intro paragraph, above the tier cards) so it stays within a 1280x800 viewport — story 002 regression guard preserved. Token-only styling throughout; reused the existing FOCUS_RING constant for WCAG 2.2 AA visible focus rings.
impl.verificationRun: grep -n 'selectedTier|activeTier|setSelectedTier' → PASS (3 matches in PhaseOrientation); grep -n 'cannedResponse|miniChat|chatPanel|cannedReplies' → PASS (cannedReplies at module scope + used in PhaseOrientation); grep -n 'stays local|works offline' → PASS (privacy note intact); grep -n 'onKeyDown|role=.button|tabIndex' → PASS (onKeyDown at line 742 inside PhaseOrientation); AC5 no <input|<textarea|<select> inside PhaseOrientation → PASS (first match at line 1167, in PhaseSeeding); npx tsc --noEmit → PASS (no errors); npm run --prefix work-hq build → PASS (built in ~470ms)
